title: 2. Available Distributions & dqrp Functions
code: # Manual inverse Gaussian sampling rinvgauss <- function(n, mu = 1, lambda = 1) {   nu <- rnorm(n)   y <- nu^2   x <- mu + (mu^2 * y) / (2 * lambda) - (mu / (2 * lambda)) * sqrt(4 * mu * lambda * y + mu^2 * y^2)   u <- runif(n)   ifelse(u <= mu / (mu + x), x, mu^2 / x) }  samples_invgauss <- rinvgauss(10000, mu = 1, lambda = 1)  df_invgauss <- data.frame(value = samples_invgauss, dist = "Inv-Gaussian(μ=1, λ=1)")  p5 <- ggplot(df_invgauss, aes(x = value)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 50, alpha = 0.6, fill = "plum") +   geom_density(color = "purple", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Inverse Gaussian Distribution", x = "Value", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 6))  print(p5) print(summary(samples_invgauss))

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: DPmixGPD uses three-phase model-building paradigm designed clarity control: Specification (compile_model_spec): Validate inputs, NIMBLE code generation Bundle (build_nimble_bundle): Generate NIMBLE code, compile sampler MCMC (run_mcmc_bundle_manual): Execute posterior sampling

title: 4. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Goal: Estimate density univariate outcome yy using nonparametric Dirichlet Process mixture Chinese Restaurant Process backend. Model: yi|G∼∫K(yi;θ)dG(θ)y_i | G \sim \int K(y_i; \theta) dG(\theta) G∼DP(α,G0)G \sim \text{DP}(\alpha, G_0) Backend: CRP truncation KmaxK_{\max} components

previous_headings: Unconditional DPmix: Chinese Restaurant Process (CRP)
title: 4. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend

# Generate from mixture of gamma distributions set.seed(42) component1 <- rgamma(60, shape = 2, rate = 1.5) component2 <- rgamma(40, shape = 1, rate = 0.5) y_mixed <- c(component1, component2)  cat("Sample size:", length(y_mixed), "
") cat("Mean:", mean(y_mixed), "

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend

what: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Goal: Estimate density univariate outcome yy extreme tail behavior using DPmix + GPD tail augmentation. Model: - Bulk (y≤uy \leq u): Nonparametric DP mixture - Tail (y>uy > u): Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) Backend: CRP bulk + GPD tail

previous_headings: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
code:
# Generate bimodal bulk + heavy tail set.seed(42) bulk_comp1 <- rgamma(80, shape = 2, rate = 1.5) bulk_comp2 <- rgamma(60, shape = 1, rate = 0.8) bulk <- c(bulk_comp1, bulk_comp2)  # Add extreme tail (GPD) tail_component <- DPmixGPD::rGpd(30, threshold = 0, scale = 1.5, shape = 0.3)  y_tail <- c(bulk, tail_component)  cat("Sample size:", length(y_tail), "
") cat("Bulk observations:", length(bulk), "
") cat("Tail observations:", length(tail_component), "
") cat("Min:", min(y_tail), "
") cat("Max:", max(y_tail), "
") cat("Mean:", mean(y_tail), "
")  # Visualization df_raw <- data.frame(y = y_tail, region = ifelse(y_tail > quantile(y_tail, 0.8), "Tail", "Bulk"))  p_raw <- ggplot(df_raw, aes(x = y, fill = region)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, alpha = 0.7) +   geom_density(alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 1) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bulk" = "steelblue", "Tail" = "red")) +   labs(title = "Data with Bulk & Tail Regions", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()  print(p_raw)

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: family splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold threshold onto Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.
